highlights.title: Claude Code v2.1.154: Opus 4.8 and Dynamic Workflows
highlights.summary: Opus 4.8 is now the default model in Claude Code, optimized for high-effort tasks — use `/effort xhigh` to push it to its limits. Dynamic Workflows let you ask Claude to orchestrate work across tens to hundreds of background agents, making it possible to tackle much larger, more complex projects than before.

highlights.title: Claude Code v2.1.157: Auto-Loading Plugins from .claude/skills
highlights.summary: Plugins placed in a `.claude/skills` directory are now loaded automatically — no marketplace setup needed. Use `claude plugin init <name>` to scaffold a new plugin quickly, and enjoy autocomplete for `/plugin` arguments to speed up your workflow.

highlights.title: Introducing Claude Opus 4.8
highlights.summary: Anthropic's official announcement covers what makes Opus 4.8 the most capable model yet, including its improved reasoning and suitability for long-horizon agentic tasks. This is the model now powering Claude Code's default experience, so understanding its strengths will help you get better results.

highlights.title: Dynamic Workflows in Claude Code
highlights.summary: This official blog post explains how Dynamic Workflows enable Claude Code to spin up and coordinate large numbers of sub-agents to tackle tasks too complex for a single session. It walks through practical use cases and how to trigger a dynamic workflow with a natural-language request.

highlights.title: Claude Code as a Daily Driver: Claude.md, Skills, Subagents, Plugins, and MCPs
highlights.summary: A comprehensive practitioner's guide covering the full Claude Code ecosystem — from crafting an effective `CLAUDE.md` file to building skills, chaining sub-agents, and integrating MCPs. Ideal for developers who have the basics down and want to level up their daily workflow.

highlights.title: Claude Code – Everything you can configure that the docs don't tell you
highlights.summary: The author dug into the Claude Code source code to surface hidden configuration options not covered in official documentation. This is a goldmine for power users who want finer control over Claude Code's behavior, including undocumented environment variables and settings.

highlights.title: Mastering Claude Code in 30 Minutes
highlights.summary: A focused 30-minute YouTube tutorial that walks through the most important Claude Code features at a pace suitable for developers who want a fast but thorough introduction. Great for getting productive quickly without watching a multi-hour course.

highlights.title: Claude Code v2.1.158: Auto Mode on Bedrock, Vertex, and Foundry
highlights.summary: Auto mode — which lets Claude intelligently select its own reasoning effort — is now available on AWS Bedrock, Google Vertex, and Foundry for Opus 4.7 and 4.8. Enable it by setting the environment variable `CLAUDE_CODE_ENABLE_AUTO_MODE=1` in your shell or CI environment.

tips_of_the_day: Create a `.claude/skills` directory in your project root and drop in small, focused plugin scripts for repetitive tasks — Claude Code will load them automatically without any marketplace setup as of v2.1.157.

Before starting a large refactor or multi-file feature, try asking Claude Code to 'create a workflow' for the task. Dynamic Workflows will break the work into parallel sub-agent jobs, often completing it significantly faster than a single linear session.

Set `CLAUDE_CODE_ENABLE_AUTO_MODE=1` in your shell profile if you use Bedrock or Vertex — Auto mode lets Claude self-select the right effort level per request, saving tokens on simple tasks while still applying full reasoning power when it matters.
